dtcs.description: Communication Circuit Error (BUS-OFF)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1000 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections, or worn/shorted wires. If the connections are good, check the 12 volt battery condition  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/12 Volt Battery Test and the charging system.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the appropriate connector at each control unit in the table. *: With HandsFreeLink system
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1000 indicated? Go to step 12. Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), then go to step 15.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P).

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P) terminal No. 6 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box connector and the affected control unit. Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models)
Reconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1000 indicated? Replace the gauge control module. Go to step 21.
Reconnect the climate control unit 36P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1000 indicated? Replace the climate control unit. - With HandsFreeLink system: go to step 27. - Without HandsFreeLink system: Replace the immobilizer-keyless control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair.
Reconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1000 indicated? Replace the HandsFreeLink control unit. Replace the immobilizer-keyless control unit.

dtcs.description: MICU Internal Error (EEPROM Error)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1002 indicated? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections. If the connections are good, check the 12 volt battery condition and the charging system.

dtcs.description: MICU Lost Communication With Gauge Control Module (VSP/NE Message)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTCs B1011 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections of the gauge control module 32P connector and at the under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P). If the connections are good, check the 12 volt battery condition and the charging system.
Measure the voltage between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 17 and body ground individually. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 8. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check the No. B1 (15 A) and the No. B22 (7.5 A) fuses in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Are the fuses OK? Repair an open or high resistance in the No. B1 (15 A) and/or No. B22 (7.5 A) fuse circuit. Replace the fuse(s). If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground in the No. B1 (15 A) and/or No. B22 (7.5 A) fuse circuit

Measure the voltage between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 4 and No. 32 and body ground individually. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G501).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P).
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P) terminal No. 6 and gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 31. Is there continuity? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Repair open or high resistance in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the gauge control module.

dtcs.description: Rear Wiper Motor (Park) Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II).
Operate the rear window wiper for at least 15 seconds, then turn the rear window wiper switch OFF.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1028 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.

Do the rear window wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test. Is the rear window wiper motor OK? Go to step 6. Replace the rear window wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair/Rear Window Wiper Motor Replacement.
Check the No. B16 (10 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 7. Replace the fuse, and recheck the system.
Disconnect the rear window wiper motor 4P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between rear window wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 10. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the rear window wiper motor.

Check for continuity between rear window wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 3 and body ground. Is there continuity? Go to step 11. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between rear window wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 3 and body ground (G801).
Disconnect the under-dash fuse/relay box connector D (49P).

Measure the voltage between rear window wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 4 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the rear window wiper motor and the under-dash fuse/relay box. Go to step 13.

Check for continuity between rear window wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 4 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the rear window wiper motor and the under-dash fuse/relay box. Go to step 14.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector D (49P) terminal No. 10 and rear window wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 4. Is there continuity? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the rear window wiper motor and the under-dash fuse/relay box.

dtcs.description: IG1 Power Supply Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1036 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections at the gauge control module 32P connector and at under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P). If the connections are good, check the 12 volt battery condition and the charging system.

Measure the voltage between gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 17 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the gauge control module.

dtcs.description: Windshield Wiper Motor (Park) Signal Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II).
Turn the windshield wiper switch to LOW or HIGH for at least 15 seconds, then turn the switch OFF. Does the wiper motor work? Go to step 4. Go to step 13.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1077 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the windshield wiper system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the windshield wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test. Does the wiper motor run and does it pulse normally? Go to step 7. Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair/Windshield Wiper Motor Replacement, and recheck.
Disconnect the windshield wiper motor 5P connector.
Disconnect the under-dash fuse/relay box connector B (36P).

Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 5P connector terminal No. 3 and under-dash fuse/relay box connector B (36P) terminal No. 9. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 5P connector terminal No. 3 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 11.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between windshield wiper motor 5P connector terminal No. 3 and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire. Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models)
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Check the No. B56 (30 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 15. Replace the fuse, and recheck the system.

Do the windshield wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test. Does the wiper motor run normally? Go to step 16. Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Service and Repair/Windshield Wiper Motor Replacement, and recheck.
Disconnect the under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (36P) and the windshield wiper motor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (36P) terminals and windshield wiper motor 5P connector terminals as shown: Is there continuity? Go to step 18. Repair an open or high resistance in the BLU (low) or GRN (high) wire.

Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 5P connector terminal No. 5 and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Repair an open in the wire or poor ground (G401).

dtcs.description: , B1079 DTC B1078: Daytime Running Light for Canada Circuit Malfunction DTC B1079: Daytime Running Light for USA Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Pull the parking brake lever.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Release the parking brake lever.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1078 or B1079 indicated? Go to step 7. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and turn the headlight switch OFF.

Check the No. B28, No. B34 [No. B48], and No. B51 fuses in the under-dash fuse/relay box. [ ]: Without HID Are all fuses OK? Go to step 9. Replace the blown fuse, and recheck. If the No. B28 (20 A) fuse is blown again, replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. If the No. B34 (10A) [No. B48 (10 A)] or No. B51 (10 A) fuse is blown again, repair a short in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the appropriate headlight (high beam).
Check the headlight bulbs. Are the headlight bulbs OK? Go to step 10. Replace the bulb and recheck.
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connectors A (36P) and B (36P).
Disconnect both of the headlight (high beam) 2P connectors.

Check for continuity between right headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2 and under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (36P) terminal No. 27. Is there continuity? Go to step 13. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the right headlight (high beam) and the under-dash fuse/relay box.

Check for continuity between left headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2 and under-dash fuse/relay box connector B (36P) terminal No. 29 [No. 20]. [ ]: Without HID With HID Without HID Is there continuity? Go to step 14. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the left headlight (high beam) and the under-dash fuse/ relay box.

Check for continuity between each headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Repair an open or high resistance in the BLK wire or poor ground (G201-right side, G401-left side).

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Pull the parking brake lever.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Release the parking brake lever.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1078 or B1079 indicated? Go to step 7. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and turn the headlight switch OFF.

Check the No. B28, No. B34 [No. B48], and No. B51 fuses in the under-dash fuse/relay box. [ ]: Without HID Are all fuses OK? Go to step 9. Replace the blown fuse, and recheck. If the No. B28 (20 A) fuse is blown again, replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. If the No. B34 (10A) [No. B48 (10 A)] or No. B51 (10 A) fuse is blown again, repair a short in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the appropriate headlight (high beam).
Check the headlight bulbs. Are the headlight bulbs OK? Go to step 10. Replace the bulb and recheck.
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connectors A (36P) and B (36P).
Disconnect both of the headlight (high beam) 2P connectors.

Check for continuity between right headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2 and under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (36P) terminal No. 27. Is there continuity? Go to step 13. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the right headlight (high beam) and the under-dash fuse/relay box.

Check for continuity between left headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2 and under-dash fuse/relay box connector B (36P) terminal No. 29 [No. 20]. [ ]: Without HID With HID Without HID Is there continuity? Go to step 14. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the left headlight (high beam) and the under-dash fuse/ relay box.

Check for continuity between each headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Repair an open or high resistance in the BLK wire or poor ground (G201-right side, G401-left side).

dtcs.description: Driver's Door Key Cylinder Switch Input Circuit Malfunction (Simultaneous input of lock and unlock signal)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Insert the ignition key into the driver's door key cylinder, and turn the key to the LOCK and UNLOCK positions at least 10 times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1127 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the driver's door key cylinder switch system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
With the driver's door key cylinder in the neutral position, select KEYLESS TRANSMITTER with the HDS, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? The MICU is faulty. Replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Go to step 7.
Disconnect the driver's door lock actuator/knob switch 10P connector.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? Faulty driver's door key cylinder switch; replace the driver's door latch  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Latch/Service and Repair. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P).

Check for continuity between body ground and under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P) terminals No. 3 (UNLOCK) and No. 4 (LOCK) individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the LOCK or UNLOCK wire. Go to step 12.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P) terminals No. 3 (UNLOCK) and No. 4 (LOCK). Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the LOCK and UNLOCK wires. Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models)

dtcs.description: Driver's Door Remote Switch Input Circuit Malfunction (Simultaneous input of lock and unlock signal)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Lock and unlock the driver's door with the driver's door lock switch.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1128 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the driver's door lock switch system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
With the driver's door lock switch in the neutral position, select KEYLESS TRANSMITTER from the HDS and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? The MICU is faulty. Replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Go to step 6.
Disconnect the power window master switch connector.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? Faulty door lock switch; replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Go to step 8.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P).

Check for continuity between body ground under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P) terminals No. 11 (LOCK) and No. 6 (UNLOCK) individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the LOCK or UNLOCK wire. Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P) terminals No. 11 (LOCK) and No. 6 (UNLOCK). Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the LOCK and UNLOCK wires. Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models)

dtcs.description: Driver's Door Knob Switch Input Circuit Malfunction (Simultaneous input of lock and unlock signal)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II).
Operate the driver's door lock knob switch several times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1129 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the driver's door lock knob switch is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Select KEYLESS TRANSMITTER from the BODY ELECTRICAL menu, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) and the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK). Are the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) information indicator ON and the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK) information indicator OFF with the driver's door lock knob switch in LOCK position, and are the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) information indicator OFF and the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK) information indicator ON with the driver's door lock knob switch in UNLOCK position? Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) Go to step 7.
Disconnect the driver's door lock actuator/knob switch 10P connector.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? Check for an open in the driver's door lock knob switch (LOCK) wire or the driver's door lock knob switch (UNLOCK) wire between the MICU and the driver's door lock knob switch. If OK, replace the driver's door latch  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Latch/Service and Repair. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P).

Check for continuity between body ground under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P) terminals No. 5 (UNLOCK) and No. 10 (LOCK) individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the LOCK or UNLOCK wire. Go to step 12.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (12P) terminals No. 5 (UNLOCK) and No. 10 (LOCK). Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the LOCK wire and UNLOCK wire. Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models)

dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Internal (EEPROM) Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1152 indicated? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the gauge control module is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back ON (II).
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTCs B1175 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the gauge control module and the fuel tank unit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the fuel tank unit 4P connector.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 21 and No. 22 and fuel tank unit 4P connector terminals No. 4 and No. 2 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Do the fuel gauge sending unit test  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Tank Unit/Testing and Inspection. Is the fuel gauge sending unit OK? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Replace fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Replacement.

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Short

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the fuel level sensor circuit is OK at this time. Check for worn/missing insulation or an internal short in the wire.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the fuel tank unit 4P connector.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 11. Replace the fuel gauge sending unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 22 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the gauge control module and the fuel tank unit. Go to step 14.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 21 and No. 22. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the gauge control module and the fuel gauge sending unit. Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Communication Bus Line Error (BUS-OFF)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

dtcs.description: Auto Light Sensor Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1575 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect automatic lighting/sunlight sensor 5P connector.
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector Q (34P).

Check for continuity between the under-dash fuse/relay box connector Q (34P) terminals and the automatic lighting/sunlight sensor 5P connector terminals as shown: Is there continuity? Go to step 8. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between automatic lighting/sunlight sensor 5P connector terminals No. 3 and No. 5. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire. Go to step 9.
Reconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector Q (34P).

Measure the voltage between body ground and under-dash fuse/relay box connector Q (34P) terminal No. 2, and between body ground and under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P) terminal No. 16. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 11. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G501, G502).
Reconnect automatic lighting/sunlight sensor 5P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between automatic lighting/sunlight sensor 5P connector terminal No. 5 and body ground. Is there about 5 V? Replace the automatic lighting/sunlight sensor  See: Lighting and Horns/Ambient Light Sensor/Service and Repair. Faulty MICU; replace the under-dash fuse/relay box. - USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (USA Models) - Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation (Canada Models) With Navigation B1775

dtcs.description: Microphone input/output shorted to power (without navigation)

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1775 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the HFL-navigation microphone 10P connector.
Disconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there voltage? There is a short to power in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the HFL-navigation microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness. Go to step 10.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 2 and HFL-navigation microphone 10P connector terminal No. 5. Is there continuity? Substitute a known-good HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair, and recheck. If the symptom goes away, replace the original HandsFreeLink control unit. If the symptom does not go away, replace the HFL-navigation microphone  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation System/Voice Activation Microphone/Service and Repair/HFL-Navigation Microphone Removal / Installation. There is an open in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the HFL-navigation microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness. Without Navigation B1775

dtcs.description: Microphone input/output shorted to ground or open (without navigation)

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1776 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the HFL-navigation microphone 10P connector.
Disconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 individually, then between terminals No. 2 and No. 3. Is there continuity? There is a short in the wire(s) between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the HFL-navigation microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 and HFL-navigation microphone 10P connector terminals No. 4 and No. 5 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. There is an open in the wire(s) between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the HFL-navigation microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness.

Remove the audio-navigation unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Service and Repair/Audio-Navigation Unit Removal/Installation, and disconnect audio-navigation unit connector D (12P).

Check for continuity between body ground and HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminals No. 15 and No. 16 individually. Is there continuity? There is a short to body ground in the wires between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the audio-navigation unit. Replace the affected shielded harness. Go to step 12.

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminals No. 15 and No. 16 and audio-navigation unit connector D (12P) terminals No. 12 and No. 11 respectively. Is there continuity? Substitute a known-good HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair, and recheck. If the symptom goes away, replace the original HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. If the symptom does not go away, replace the faulty audio-navigation unit. There is an open in the wires between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the audio-navigation unit. Replace the affected shielded harness. Without Navigation B1776

dtcs.description: HandsFreeLink steering wheel switch failure (without navigation)

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), then start the vehicle and turn the steering wheel back and forth several times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1779 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the HFL switch test. Is the switch OK? Go to step 7. Replace the HFL-navigation voice control switch  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Sensors and Switches - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Switch/Service and Repair/Voice Control Switch Replacement.
Disconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector.
Disconnect the HFL-navigation voice control switch 5P connector, then connect cable reel connector A (20P) and C (20P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between HFL-navigation voice control switch 5P connector terminal No. 3 and body ground. Is there voltage? Go to step 13. Go to step 11.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and HFL-navigation voice control switch 5P connector terminal No. 3. Is there continuity? Go to step 15. Repair an open in the wire between the HFL-navigation voice control switch, the cable reel, and the HandsFreeLink control unit.
Disconnect cable reel connector A (20P).

Measure the voltage between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the cable reel. Replace the cable reel.

Remove the audio-navigation unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Service and Repair/Audio-Navigation Unit Removal/Installation, and disconnect audio-navigation unit connector A (24P).

Check for continuity between HFL-navigation voice control switch 5P connector terminal No. 2 and audio-navigation unit connector A (24P) terminal No. 11. Is there continuity? Replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. Go to step 17.

Substitute a known-good cable reel, and check for continuity between HFL-navigation voice control switch 5P connector terminal No. 2 and audio-navigation unit connector A (24P) terminal No. 11. Is there continuity? Replace the original cable reel  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair. Repair an open in the wire between the audio remote, the HFL-navigation voice control switch, and the audio-navigation unit. Without Navigation B1779

dtcs.description: HandsFreeLink steering wheel switch line short (without navigation)

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), then start the vehicle, and turn the steering wheel back and forth several times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1780 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the HFL switch test. Is the switch OK? Go to step 7. Replace the HFL-navigation voice control switch  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Sensors and Switches - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Switch/Service and Repair/Voice Control Switch Replacement.
Disconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector.
Disconnect the HFL-navigation voice control switch 5P connector.

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit and the HFL-navigation voice control switch. Replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. Without Navigation B1780

dtcs.description: HandsFreeLink control unit module error

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1792 indicated? Replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Communication Circuit Error (BUS-OFF)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1900 indicated? Troubleshoot DTC B1000. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: , B1902 DTC B1901: Immobilizer Unit Internal Error (CPU Error) DTC B1902: Immobilizer Unit Internal Error (EEPROM Error)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1901 or B1902 indicated? Replace the immobilizer-keyless control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair . Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: B1901, B1902 DTC B1901: Immobilizer Unit Internal Error (CPU Error) DTC B1902: Immobilizer Unit Internal Error (EEPROM Error)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1901 or B1902 indicated? Replace the immobilizer-keyless control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair . Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Immobilizer Unit Lost Communication With MICU (Door Lock Switch Message)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1905 indicated? Faulty immobilizer-keyless control unit; replace the immobilizer-keyless control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair. Intermittent failure, the immobilizer-keyless control unit is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the immobilizer-keyless control unit and the MICU.

dtcs.description: Immobilizer Unit Lost Communication With Gauge Control Module (A/T Message)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1906 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the gauge control module and the immobilizer-keyless control unit.
From the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, select B-CAN CONTROL UNITS INFORMATION, and then select CHECK CONNECTED CONTROL UNITS. Is the gauge control module detected? Go to step 8. Go to step 6.

Measure the voltage between body ground and gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 17 individually. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 7. Check the No. B1 (15 A) and the No. B22 (7.5 A) fuses in the under-dash fuse/relay box. If the fuse is blown, replace the fuse and recheck the DTCs. If the fuses are OK, repair an open in the wire between the under-dash fuse/relay box and the gauge control module.

Measure the voltage between body ground and gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 4 and No. 32 individually. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 8. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire or poor ground (G501).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P).

Check for continuity between immobilizer-keyless control unit 7P connector terminal No. 4 and gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 31. Is there continuity? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator J and AUTO: An Open in the Humidity Sensor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B2967 or J and AUTO indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the humidity sensor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Remove the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair, and test it  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Humidity Sensor/Testing and Inspection. Is the humidity sensor OK? With the humidity/in-car temperature sensor disconnected, go to step 7. Replace the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair.
Disconnect the climate control unit 36P connector.
Disconnect the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector and air mix control motor 7P connector.

Check for continuity between the following terminals of the climate control unit 36P connector and the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector. Is there continuity? Check for loose wires or poor connections at the climate control unit 36P connector and at the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit and the humidity sensor.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator K and AUTO: A Short in the Humidity Sensor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B2968 or K and AUTO indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the humidity sensor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the climate control unit 36P connector.
Disconnect the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector and air mix control motor 7P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and climate control unit 36P connector terminals No. 12, 19, and No. 35 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire(s) between the climate control unit and the humidity sensor. Go to step 9.
Check for continuity between terminals of the climate control unit 36P connector as follows. Is there continuity between any of the terminals? Repair a short in the wires. Go to step 10.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and check the same terminals for voltage to body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire(s) between the climate control unit and the humidity sensor. This short may also damage the climate control unit. Repair the short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Go to step 11.
Reconnect the humidity/in-car temperature sensor 4P connector and air mix control motor 7P connector.
Reconnect the climate control unit 36P connector.

Test the humidity sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Humidity Sensor/Testing and Inspection. Is the humidity temperature sensor OK? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Replace the humidity/in-car temperature sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Climate Control Unit Lost Communication with MICU (WIPSW message)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B2969 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections at the gauge control module and in the climate control unit circuit.
Select B-CAN Control Units Information in the BODY ELECTRICAL menu.

Select Check Connected Control Units in the B-CAN Control Units Information menu. Is the gauge control module detected? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Go to step 7.
Disconnect under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P).
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between under-dash fuse/relay box connector Y (16P) terminal No. 6 and gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 31. Is there continuity? Go to the gauge control module input test  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics. Repair an open in the wire between the MICU and the gauge control module.
